title: allium carinatum subsp. violaceum
intro: Allium carinatum subsp. violaceum, also known as the keel-fruit garlic or purple keel garlic, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Allium, native to Europe. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, antiviral, and antifungal properties, making it a natural remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of Allium carinatum subsp. violaceum include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and improve respiratory health. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in traditional medicine to treat colds, flu, and other infections.
